role: Senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy; specialist on Iran and Gulf defense, ballistic missiles, and IRGC military capabilities
bio:
Farzin Nadimi is a London-based defense and security analyst who serves as a senior fellow at the [Washington Institute for Near East Policy](/orgs/washington-institute-for-near-east-policy). He specializes in Iran and Gulf security, with particular focus on the [IRGC](/orgs/islamic-revolutionary-guard-corps)'s military capabilities, Iran's ballistic missile and drone programs, and the regional projection of Iranian military power.

Nadimi has produced some of the most technically detailed open-source analysis of Iran's missile tests, including the Shahab-3 and Sejjil programs, and has tracked the IRGC's weapons transfers to regional proxies including Hezbollah and the Houthis. His work is widely cited by policymakers, journalists, and intelligence analysts seeking to understand the operational dimensions of Iran's defense posture. He has testified before the U.S. Congress and briefed European governments on Iran's military programs.

sources.title: Farzin Nadimi — Washington Institute for Near East Policy

editorial_notes: London-based. Technical analyst of IRGC capabilities. Senior fellow at WINEP.
